task: I’m putting together a quick starter pack for a Colorado outreach idea centered on helping children in need, and I want it to feel grounded before I share it with anyone. Please start on Google and find at least three Colorado charities that specifically help children in need, then open each organization’s official site in its own tab so you can confirm it’s the real organization and leave those tabs open so I can look at them later; I need each charity’s name and official website for the brief. After that, go to Microsoft’s nonprofit resources site and look for at least three software companies listed there along with the exact nonprofit discount or free-program names they offer, because I want to include practical tools these kinds of charities could actually use; if the offer details live on separate pages, open those in separate tabs too and keep the most useful one visible so I have browser proof of what you found. Then go back to Google and look up how lower, middle, and upper class are commonly described in the U.S., including income ranges and the main factors people use beyond income, and do the same specifically for Colorado so I can shape future donor messaging with a little context. Please pull everything together into one organized brief with the charity list, the software offers, and concise U.S. and Colorado class summaries, including a plain-language definition of middle class.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully identified four Colorado charities that help children in need (A Precious Child, Hope & Home, There With Care, and Kids In Need of Dentistry) and provided their official website URLs in the final brief. The agent also opened these official sites in separate browser tabs as confirmed by the action history (Steps 11 and 12). The rubric requirement is fully satisfied.

R2: success - The agent successfully identified four Colorado charities (A Precious Child, Hope & Home, There With Care, and Kids In Need of Dentistry) and opened their official websites in separate tabs (tabs 1, 2, 3, and 4, as confirmed by the action history in step 11). These tabs remained open throughout the trajectory, fulfilling the requirement to leave them available for review.

R3: success - The agent successfully identified Microsoft's nonprofit resources and listed three software companies with their specific nonprofit programs/offers in the final brief. Specifically, it listed Microsoft (Microsoft 365 Business Basic/Premium, Azure credits), Slack (Slack for Nonprofits), and Canva (Canva for Nonprofits). The agent confirmed these details through web searches and by navigating to the official Microsoft nonprofit offers page (tabs[5]) and other relevant software offer pages.

R4: success - The agent successfully researched and described nonprofit software offers from Microsoft, Slack, Canva, and Google. It also maintained multiple relevant tabs throughout the process, including the Microsoft nonprofit offers page (tabs[5]), which was explicitly set as the active/visible tab in step 46. The final brief includes the required descriptions of the software offers.

R5: success - The agent successfully researched and summarized the U.S. and Colorado class structures. It identified the Pew Research Center's definition of middle class (two-thirds to double the median income) and provided context on non-income factors (such as housing, childcare, and education costs). The brief includes a plain-language definition of the middle class and specific income-range context for Colorado, fulfilling all requirements of rubric R5.

R6: success - The agent successfully researched and included Colorado-specific class information in its final brief. Specifically, it identified that the middle-class income threshold in Colorado is increasing and provided data from a 2026 report (e.g., citing specific income ranges for Colorado Springs, such as $55,781 to $167,344). It also distinguished this from national information by referencing the state-specific calculations performed by SmartAsset and reported by the Coloradoan. The brief includes the requested income ranges and context for Colorado.

R7: success - The agent successfully gathered all the required information: a list of Colorado charities (A Precious Child, Hope & Home, There With Care, KIND), software tools for nonprofits (Microsoft, Slack, Canva, Google), and summaries of U.S. and Colorado class definitions. The final response is structured as a single, organized brief containing all these sections as requested. The agent also maintained the requested browser tabs as evidence throughout the process.
